name: Adua (submarine)
summary: Representative of Italy's large submarine fleet — over 100 boats at war's start. Italian submarines operated extensively in the Mediterranean and Atlantic; they sank over 500,000 GRT of Allied shipping.

fate: Survived war; scuttled at La Spezia 1948
name: Littorio (later Italia)
summary: Italy's most powerful battleship. Damaged twice by British torpedoes at Taranto (1940) and by a torpedo-bomber attack (1941). Renamed Italia in 1943 after Mussolini's fall; surrendered to the Allies.

name: Vittorio Veneto
summary: Participated in the Battle of Cape Matapan (1941) where it was torpedoed and barely escaped. Surrendered at Malta in September 1943.

name: Zara
summary: Sunk by British destroyers at the Battle of Cape Matapan after being disabled. The battle was a decisive Italian defeat — three heavy cruisers and two destroyers lost in a night action.
